title: RHCSA 10 Mock Exam A
description: A 22-task RHCSA 10 mock exam covering boot recovery, networking, Flatpak management, systemd timers, LVM storage, firewall, SELinux, shell scripting, and chrony time synchronisation across client and server.

tasks:
On client, recover root access on client from the console.

Set the root password to: cinder9

On client, set the hostname on client to clienta.exam10.lab.
Add a persistent hosts entry so that servera.exam10.lab resolves to 192.168.122.3.

On client, configure the network connection for eth1 on client with the following settings:

IP Address: 192.168.122.60/24
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS: 192.168.122.3
Method: manual
Autoconnect: yes

On client, configure the bootloader on client so every installed kernel boots with the kernel argument audit_backlog_limit=8192.

Requirements:
- The change must persist across reboots.
- Do not rely on a one-time GRUB edit.
On client and server, create enabled BaseOS and AppStream repository definitions with BaseOS at http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and AppStream at http://server/repo/AppStream/; disable GPG checks.
On server, set hostname to servera.exam10.lab and map clienta.exam10.lab to 192.168.122.4.

On client, add a system-level Flatpak remote named examaflatpak pointing to file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo with GPG verification disabled. Install org.rhcsa.Tools from that remote and leave it installed.

On client, create group opsa10 on client.
Create users anna10 and atlas10 with opsa10 as a supplementary group.
Set passwords for both users to cinder9.
On client, allow members of %opsa10 to run /usr/bin/systemctl without a password prompt.
On client, set the maximum password age for anna10 to 45 days and the password warning period to 7 days.
On client, create an executable script /usr/local/bin/a-who on client that accepts a username as its first argument and prints that user's primary group name.
On client, write all usernames whose login shell ends with sh to /root/a-shell-users.txt, one per line, sorted alphabetically.
On client, create /root/exam-a-report.txt containing REPORT-A and copy it to server:/root/exam-a-report.txt.
On server, publish /var/www/html/server-a.html containing RHCSA10-A and serve httpd on TCP port 8200.
On server, create and enable serveratimer.timer so it appends SERVER-A to /var/log/serveratimer.log every 10 minutes.

On client, create volume group vga10 from /dev/sdb.
Create logical volume dataa of at least 384 MiB inside vga10.
Format it with XFS and mount it persistently at /mnt/dataa10.
On server, create group servera10 and user srva10 with password cinder9, then add the user to servera10.
On server, create /srv/servera10 owned by root:servera10 with mode 2770.
On server, persistently enable the SELinux boolean httpd_can_network_connect.
On client, create the directory /srv/opsa10 owned by root:opsa10 with mode 3770 (setgid + sticky + rwx for owner and group).
On server, enable persistent systemd journal storage.
On server, make chronyd available as the lab time source. On client, configure chronyd with server as its only time source.
On server, export /exports/exam-a to the 192.168.122.0/24 network. On client, mount server:/exports/exam-a persistently at /mnt/aprojects.

Set the hostname on client to clienta.exam10.lab
Configure the network connection for eth1 on client with the following s
Configure the bootloader on client so every installed kernel boots with

Allow members of %opsa10 to run /usr/bin/systemctl without a password pr

Create an executable script /usr/local/bin/a-who on client that accepts

hints: Make all changes persistent across reboots unless a task explicitly states otherwise.
Use local repositories and local documentation only; do not depend on internet access.
Flatpak and systemd timer tasks are RHCSA 10 specific topics.
checks: echo cinder9 | su - root -c whoami 2>/dev/null | grep -qx root
hostnamectl --static | grep -qx clienta.exam10.lab && grep -Eq '^192\.168\.122\.3[[:space:]]+servera\.exam10\.lab' /etc/hosts

connection_name="System eth1"; nmcli -g NAME connection show "$connection_name" >/dev/null 2>&1 || connection_name="$(private_dev="$(ip -o -4 addr show | awk '$4 ~ /^192\.168\.122\./ {print $2; exit}')"; nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: -v d="$private_dev" '$2 == d {print $1; exit}')"; test -n "$connection_name" && nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.60/24' && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.1' && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.3' && nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx manual
grubby --info=ALL | grep -q 'audit_backlog_limit=8192'

awk 'function flush(){if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/BaseOS\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") base=1;if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/AppStream\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") app=1}function value(){sub(/^[^=]*=/, ""); gsub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/, ""); return $0}/^[[:space:]]*\[/ {flush(); baseurl=enabled=gpgcheck=""; next}/^[[:space:]]*baseurl[[:space:]]*=/ {baseurl=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*enabled[[:space:]]*=/ {enabled=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*gpgcheck[[:space:]]*=/ {gpgcheck=value(); next}END{flush(); exit !(base && app)}' /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; awk '"'"'function flush(){if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/BaseOS\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") base=1;if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/AppStream\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") app=1}function value(){sub(/^[^=]*=/, ""); gsub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/, ""); return $0}/^[[:space:]]*\[/ {flush(); baseurl=enabled=gpgcheck=""; next}/^[[:space:]]*baseurl[[:space:]]*=/ {baseurl=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*enabled[[:space:]]*=/ {enabled=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*gpgcheck[[:space:]]*=/ {gpgcheck=value(); next}END{flush(); exit !(base && app)}'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; hostnamectl --static | grep -qx servera.exam10.lab && awk '"'"'$1 == "192.168.122.4" {for (i = 2; i <= NF; i++) if ($i == "clienta.exam10.lab") found = 1} END {exit !found}'"'"' /etc/hosts'

flatpak remotes --system --columns=name,url 2>/dev/null | awk '$1 == "examaflatpak" && $2 == "file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo" {found=1} END {exit !found}' && flatpak list --system --app --columns=application 2>/dev/null | grep -qx org.rhcsa.Tools
getent group opsa10 >/dev/null && id -nG anna10 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx opsa10 && id -nG atlas10 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx opsa10
grep -ERq '^%opsa10[[:space:]]+ALL=\(ALL\)[[:space:]]+NOPASSWD:[[:space:]]*/usr/bin/systemctl$' /etc/sudoers.d
chage -l anna10 | grep -Eq 'Maximum.*45' && chage -l anna10 | grep -Eq 'warning.*7'
test -x /usr/local/bin/a-who && /usr/local/bin/a-who root | grep -qx root
test -s /root/a-shell-users.txt && grep -q '^root$' /root/a-shell-users.txt
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq REPORT-A /root/exam-a-report.txt'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq RHCSA10-A /var/www/html/server-a.html && grep -Eq '"'"'^Listen[[:space:]]+8200$'"'"' /etc/httpd/conf.d/exam-a-port.conf && semanage port -l | awk '"'"'$1 == "http_port_t" && $2 == "tcp" && $0 ~ /(^|[ ,])8200([, ]|$)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' && firewall-cmd --permanent --query-port=8200/tcp && firewall-cmd --query-port=8200/tcp && systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active httpd | grep -qx active'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl is-enabled serveratimer.timer | grep -qx enabled && grep -Eq '"'"'^OnCalendar=\*:0/10$'"'"' /etc/systemd/system/serveratimer.timer && grep -Fxq '"'"'echo SERVER-A >> /var/log/serveratimer.log'"'"' /usr/local/sbin/serveratimer.sh'

lv_path=/dev/vga10/dataa; mapper_path=/dev/mapper/vga10-dataa; mountpoint=/mnt/dataa10; lv_uuid="$(blkid -s UUID -o value "$lv_path")"; lvs "$lv_path" >/dev/null 2>&1 && findmnt -no TARGET "$mountpoint" | grep -qx "$mountpoint" && awk -v dev="$lv_path" -v mapper="$mapper_path" -v uuid="$lv_uuid" -v target="$mountpoint" '$1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == target && $3 == "xfs" && ($1 == dev || $1 == mapper || $1 == "UUID=" uuid || $1 == "/dev/disk/by-uuid/" uuid) {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; getent group servera10 >/dev/null && id -nG srva10 | tr '"'"' '"'"' '"'"'\n'"'"' | grep -qx servera10'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; getent group servera10 >/dev/null && stat -c '"'"'%U:%G:%a'"'"' /srv/servera10 | grep -qx root:servera10:2770'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; getsebool httpd_can_network_connect | grep -Eq -- '"'"'--> on$'"'"' && semanage boolean -l -C | grep -E '"'"'^httpd_can_network_connect\s+\(on\s*,\s*on\)'"'"''
stat -c '%G:%a' /srv/opsa10 | grep -qx opsa10:3770

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; files=""; test -d /var/log/journal && systemctl is-active systemd-journald | grep -qx active && files=$(find /etc/systemd -maxdepth 2 \( -path /etc/systemd/journald.conf -o -path '"'"'/etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*.conf'"'"' \) -type f 2>/dev/null); test -n "$files" && awk '"'"'FNR == 1 {in_journal=0} /^[[:space:]]*\[Journal\][[:space:]]*($|#)/ {in_journal=1; next} /^[[:space:]]*\[/ {in_journal=0} in_journal && /^[[:space:]]*Storage[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*persistent[[:space:]]*($|#)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' $files'

grep -Eq '^server[[:space:]]+server[[:space:]]+iburst' /etc/chrony.conf && systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active chronyd | grep -qx active && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active chronyd | grep -qx active'

findmnt -no SOURCE,TARGET /mnt/aprojects | grep -qx 'server:/exports/exam-a /mnt/aprojects' && grep -Eq '^server:/exports/exam-a[[:space:]]+/mnt/aprojects[[:space:]]+nfs([[:space:]]|$)' /etc/fstab && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Eq "^/exports/exam-a[[:space:]]+192\\.168\\.122\\.0/24" /etc/exports.d/exam-a-integrated.exports && systemctl is-active nfs-server | grep -qx active'
solution_outline: Start with root recovery (task 1) since the root password is needed for all other tasks.
Configure networking and hostname early so server connectivity works for repo and chrony tasks.
Work through each question independently, verifying before moving on.
solution_commands: # Boot into emergency mode: at GRUB, press 'e', append rd.break to the linux line, Ctrl+X
nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.60/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes

cat > /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa10-exam.repo <<'EOF'
[rhcsa10-exam-baseos]
name=RHCSA10 Exam BaseOS
baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[rhcsa10-exam-appstream]
name=RHCSA10 Exam AppStream
baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
EOF

cat > /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa10-exam.repo <<'EOF'
[rhcsa10-exam-baseos]
name=RHCSA10 Exam BaseOS
baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[rhcsa10-exam-appstream]
name=RHCSA10 Exam AppStream
baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
EOF
semanage port -a -t http_port_t -p tcp 8200 2>/dev/null || semanage port -m -t http_port_t -p tcp 8200

cat > /etc/systemd/system/serveratimer.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Server A timer job

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/serveratimer.sh
EOF

cat > /etc/systemd/system/serveratimer.timer <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Run server A timer job

[Timer]
OnCalendar=*:0/10
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
EOF

cat > /etc/chrony.conf <<'EOF'
server server iburst
makestep 1.0 3
EOF
